channel.title: Introduction on Temporary Escape
channel.description: Recent content in Introduction on Temporary Escape

item.title: Libraries
item.description: Libraries # List of libraries used in this game. wren - Wren, an embeddable scripting language. Used for scripting purposes and modding. wrenbind17 - A header only library for binding C++17 to Wren language. nuklear - A single-header ANSI C immediate mode cross-platform GUI library. zlib - Needed by libpng. glfw3 - Window and OpenGL context management. glm - OpenGL header only C++ mathematics library.

item.title: Overview
item.description: Architecture Overview # Here is a rough diagram of architecture and component ownership. For example, The Application is responsible for the AssetManager. More details of each component is described further down in this page. To have more in-depth information, it is highly recommended to start with the networking page. mermaid.initialize({ "theme": "dark" }) flowchart LR Main -- Application Application -- Renderer Application -- AssetManager Application -- ModManager Application -- Server Server -- Sector Sector -- Scene Server -- Database Server -- Services Server -- TCP-Server Application -- Client Client -- TCP-Client Client -- Scene-Proxy Client -- Store/Cache Application -- Views TCP-Server -- *TCP* TCP-Client -- *TCP* Example of an asteroid # The following is an example of data flow, and what roughly happens, when we want to display a simple scene with an entity (such as an steroid) to the player once they connect to the server.

item.title: VS Code
item.description: Visual Studio Code # This setup is suitable for Windows, Linux, or Mac OSX. 1. Dependencies # Make sure that you have installed dependencies and installed VSCode. 2. Cloning # Clone https://github.com/matusnovak/temporary-escape.git (or your own fork) via command line or through the GitHub VSCode extension. Open the temporary-folder through VS Code. You will also need to download the git submodules. git clone https://github.com/matusnovak/temporary-escape.git cd temporary-escape git submodule update --init 3.

item.title: CLion
item.description: JetBrains CLion # This setup is suitable for Windows, Linux, or Mac OSX. 1. Dependencies # Make sure that you have installed dependencies and installed CLion. 2. Cloning # Clone https://github.com/matusnovak/temporary-escape.git (or your own fork) via command line or through the “Get from VCS” option within the IDE. You will also need to download the git submodules. git clone https://github.com/matusnovak/temporary-escape.git cd temporary-escape git submodule update --init 3. Open the project # Launch CLion and open the cloned repository.

item.title: Networking
item.description: Networking # Client Server model # The game works in a classic client-server model. Server is responsible for simulating the world and persisting the data. The client is responsible for reading the information provided by the server and render it. There is no lockstep protocol or any similar mechanism that you may find in an FPS game. The client sends “commands” to the server. One of such command can be “approach this enemy NPC” while you are in your own player ship.

item.title: Server
item.description: Server # Tick # TBA Sectors and scenes # TBA Next # See Asset Manager page.

item.title: Visual Studio
item.description: Microsoft Visual Studio # This setup is available only for Windows. 1. Dependencies # Make sure that you have installed dependencies and installed Visual Studio. 2. Cloning # Clone https://github.com/matusnovak/temporary-escape.git (or your own fork) via command line or through the Visual Studio. You will also need to download the git submodules. git clone https://github.com/matusnovak/temporary-escape.git cd temporary-escape git submodule update --init 3. Open the project # Open Visual Studio and open the cloned repository by choosing “Open a local folder” option.

item.title: Asset Manager
item.description: Asset Manager # Loading # TBA Modding support # TBA Types of assets # TBA Next # See Database page.

item.title: Database
item.description: Database # RocksDB # TBA Serialization # TBA Operations # TBA Next # See Entity Component System page.

item.title: C++ Dependencies
item.description: C++ Dependencies # In order to build this game from the source you will need the following tools: git Clang compiler with C++17 support or MSVC vcpkg latest version cmake version 3.10 or higher Some additional OS dependencies MinGW or Cygwin Neither MinGW or Cygwin are officially supported. They might work but it is recommended to use Clang or MSVC. Vcpkg must be installed through git

item.title: Building
item.description: Building # Manually building is boring. It is recommended that you use one of the IDEs as described here. 1. Prerequisites # Make sure that you have installed dependencies 2. Cloning # Clone this project including its git submodules. git clone https://github.com/matusnovak/temporary-escape.git git submodule update --init 3. Configure project # Last argument is a dot Don’t forget to specify which folder the CMake should run the configuration from.
